file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive integration tests for authentication flow
test_coverage_areas: User status validation (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, INVITED, DELETED)

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Django REST Framework's APIClient for HTTP-like requests to API endpoints
patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows testing of API endpoints without spinning up server

patterns_discovered.description: Centralized URL path definitions via helper functions (get_login_url, get_logout_url, etc.)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces URL string duplication and makes maintenance easier if endpoints change

patterns_discovered.description: Uses factory classes (UserFactory, OrganizationFactory) to create consistent test data
patterns_discovered.benefit: Provides reproducible test fixtures with sensible defaults

patterns_discovered.description: Tests multiple user/organization states (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, INVITED, DELETED)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Comprehensive validation of business logic across different entity states

patterns_discovered.description: Verifies both API responses and database state changes (session creation, last_login_at updates)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures side effects are properly persisted

patterns_discovered.description: Tests JWT token flow including generation, refresh, and verification

gotchas_discovered.description: Email login is case-insensitive, which could be a security/UX consideration
gotchas_discovered.implication: Users may be confused about email format or potential security implications

gotchas_discovered.description: User cannot login if their organization is suspended, even if user status is ACTIVE
gotchas_discovered.implication: Implicit dependency on organization status that may not be obvious to users

gotchas_discovered.description: Soft-deleted users cannot login, indicating soft delete is a valid login gate
gotchas_discovered.implication: Need to ensure soft delete behavior is consistent across the application

gotchas_discovered.description: Sessions have explicit status tracking (ACTIVE/REVOKED) rather than relying solely on token blacklist
gotchas_discovered.implication: Dual mechanism for tracking session state - need to keep both in sync

gotchas_discovered.description: Login updates user's last_login_at field automatically
gotchas_discovered.implication: Could cause unexpected writes on every login; consider performance implications at scale

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive integration test suite for authentication flow
key_achievements: Created 957-line test file covering complete auth lifecycle
Implemented tests for 7+ distinct functionality areas
Used factory pattern for consistent test data generation
Centralized API endpoint URLs via helper functions
Tested both happy path and error scenarios
Verified database state changes alongside API responses
Covered edge cases (case-insensitive email, suspended org, soft delete)
approach_outcome.test_strategy: Integration-level testing using APIClient to test full request/response cycle with real database
approach_outcome.coverage_approach: Black-box testing of authentication endpoints with focus on business logic and state transitions

recommendations.recommendation: Consider using pytest fixtures to share common setup (login flow, user creation) across test classes to reduce duplication
recommendations.rationale: Would reduce boilerplate and make tests more maintainable

recommendations.recommendation: Add edge case tests for concurrent login/logout operations and race conditions in session management
recommendations.rationale: Authentication systems are critical for security; concurrent operations are real-world scenarios

recommendations.recommendation: Add tests for token expiration, invalid/expired tokens, and token manipulation attempts
recommendations.rationale: Critical security aspects of JWT implementation need explicit validation

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor database query count in tests using django.test.utils.override_settings to detect N+1 query problems
recommendations.rationale: Login endpoint is high-traffic; query efficiency is important

recommendations.recommendation: The module docstring is excellent; consider adding docstring comments to complex test scenarios explaining business logic
recommendations.rationale: Helps future developers understand 'why' not just 'what' tests are checking

recommendations.recommendation: Consider extracting response assertions into helper methods to standardize validation patterns across tests
recommendations.rationale: Would improve code consistency and make response structure changes easier to update
